rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.,DBA GREAT VS. OAKLAND OPERA THEATER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 16, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT THOMAS DEAN MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND The motion to vacate the judgment against Mr. Dean is granted as unopposed. Plaintiff does not object to that relief. Defendant Dean's motion to dismiss this action based on CCP 583.210 is denied. Plaintiff obtained judgment against Mr. Dean based on CCP 187. Under that procedure, Oakland Opera Theater and Mr. Dean are alter egos and the Court was merely inserting the correct name of the real defendant. See McClellan v. Northridge Park Townhome Owners Ass'n, (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 746, 752. The current record does not demonstrate that plaintiff needed to serve a new summons on Mr. Dean. The issues regarding whether plaintiff properly served Oakland Opera Theater and whether the statute of frauds or statute of limitations bars this action are not properly before the Court at this time. The Court denies Mr. Dean's fee request per CCP 128.5. That statute does not apply to this action because it was filed after December 31, 1994. See CCP 128.5(b)(1). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 16, 2013, Line 29, DEFENDANT AGILE 1, A CALIFORNIA COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Overruled. Pegastaff has pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for conspiracy. (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 511.) The pleadings are not uncertain. Pegastaff's introduction of argument and evidence for the first time in reply is improper. rulings.Case Title: MARK BONIN et al VS. BURGER BAR SAN FRANCISCO LLC., A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatoriesn - General, Form Interrogaories - Employment Law, Request For Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 16, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFFs MARK BONIN, AMANDA ROSE, KENNETH KOLICH, BROOKE MAGUIRE and TARA GALLEGOS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - General, Form Interrogaories - Employment Law, Request For Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THELMA FLORES-ARROYO VS. NORMAN YUNG DMD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compellin Defts Responses And Further Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 16, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 5, PLAINTIFF THELMA FLORES-ARROYO's Motion For Order Compelling Defendants Responses And Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Set Of Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)
